","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.83.content.1":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"SearchDex","formats":[{"type":"id","generated":true,"id":"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.83.content.1.formats.0","typename":"LinkFormat"}]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.83.content.1.formats.0":{"__typename":"LinkFormat","url":"http:\u002F\u002Fweb.archive.org\u002Fweb\u002F20210503143815\u002Fhttp:\u002F\u002Fwww.searchdex.com\u002F","title":"Its site."},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.83.content.2":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":". Executives there did not return e-mail or phone calls.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.85.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Penney also issued a statement: “We are disappointed that Google has reduced our rankings due to this matter,” Ms. Brossart wrote, “but we will continue to work actively to retain our high natural search position.”","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.87.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"She added that while the collection of links surely brought in additional revenue, it was hardly a bonanza. Just 7 percent of JCPenney.com’s traffic comes from clicks on organic search results, she wrote. A far bigger source of profits this holiday season, she stated, came from partnerships with companies like Yahoo and Time Warner, from new mobile applications and from in-store kiosks.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.89.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Search experts, however, say Penney likely reaped substantial rewards from the paid links. If you think of Google as the entrance to the planet’s largest shopping center, the links helped Penney appear as though it was the first and most inviting spot in the mall, to millions and millions of online shoppers.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.91.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"How valuable was that? ","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.91.content.1":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"A study","formats":[{"type":"id","generated":true,"id":"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.91.content.1.formats.0","typename":"LinkFormat"}]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.91.content.1.formats.0":{"__typename":"LinkFormat","url":"http:\u002F\u002Fweb.archive.org\u002Fweb\u002F20210503143815\u002Fhttp:\u002F\u002Finsights.chitika.com\u002F2010\u002Fthe-value-of-google-result-positioning\u002F","title":"A post about the study."},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.91.content.2":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":" last May by Daniel Ruby of Chitika, an online advertising network of 100,000 sites, found that, on average, 34 percent of Google’s traffic went to the No. 1 result, about twice the percentage that went to No. 2.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.93.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"The ","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.93.content.1":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Keyword Estimator","formats":[{"type":"id","generated":true,"id":"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.93.content.1.formats.0","typename":"LinkFormat"}]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.93.content.1.formats.0":{"__typename":"LinkFormat","url":"http:\u002F\u002Fweb.archive.org\u002Fweb\u002F20210503143815\u002Fhttps:\u002F\u002Fadwords.google.com\u002Fselect\u002FTrafficEstimatorSandbox","title":"The tool."},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.93.content.2":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":" at Google puts the number of searches for “dresses” in the United States at 11.1 million a month, an average based on 12 months of data. So for “dresses” alone, Penney may have been attracting roughly 3.8 million visits every month it showed up as No. 1. Exactly how many of those visits translate into sales, and the size of each sale, only Penney would know.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.95.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"But in January, the company was crowing about its online holiday sales. Kate Coultas, a company spokeswoman, wrote to a reporter in January, “Internet sales through jcp.com posted strong growth in December, with significant increases in traffic and orders for the key holiday shopping periods of the week after Thanksgiving and the week before Christmas.”","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.97.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"There was considerable pressure from investors for Penney to deliver strong holiday results. It has been struggling through one of the more trying times of its century of retailing. The $17.8 billion in revenue it reported last year is the exact same figure it reported in 2001. It announced in January that it would close a handful of underperforming stores, as well as two of its five call centers and 19 outlets that sell excess catalog merchandise.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.99.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Adding to the company’s woes is the demise of its catalog business. Penney has phased out what it called its Big Book and poured money into its Web site. But so far, the loss of the catalog has not been offset by the expansion of the Web site. At its peak, the catalog brought in about $4 billion in revenue. In 2009, the site brought in $1.5 billion.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.101.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"“For the last 35 years, Penney has tried to be accepted as a department store, and during unusually good times, it does very well,” said Bernard Sosnick, an analyst at Gilford Securities. “But in bad times, it gets punished by shoppers who pull back after having spent aspirationally.”","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.103.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"MANY owners of Web sites with Penney links seem to relish their unreachability. But there were exceptions, and they included cocaman.ch. (“Geekness — closer to the world” is the cryptic header atop the site.) It turned out to be owned and run by Corsin Camichel, a chatty 25-year-old I.T. security analyst in Switzerland.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.105.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"The word “dresses” appears in a small collection of links in the middle of a largely blank Cocaman page. Asked about that link, Mr. Camichel said his records show that it turned up on his site last April, though he said it might have been earlier than that.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.107.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"The link came through a Web site, TNX.net, which pays Mr. Camichel with TNX points, which he then trades for links that drive traffic to his other sites, like cookingutensils.net. He earns money when people visit that site and click on the ads. He could also, he said, get cash from TNX. Currently, Cocaman is home to 403 links, all of them placed there by TNX on behalf of clients.","formats":[]},"Image:SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvN2NmODY3OGUtNTA1YS01NTVjLTg5MjAtODhiOTAwZGExYmZk":{"id":"SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvN2NmODY3OGUtNTA1YS01NTVjLTg5MjAtODhiOTAwZGExYmZk","imageType":"photo","url":"http:\u002F\u002Fweb.archive.org\u002Fweb\u002F20210503143815\u002Fhttps:\u002F\u002Fwww.nytimes.com\u002Fimagepages\u002F2011\u002F02\u002F13\u002Fbusiness\u002FJP-SEARCH-2.html","uri":"nyt:\u002F\u002Fimage\u002F7cf8678e-505a-555c-8920-88b900da1bfd","credit":"Thor Swift for The New York Times","legacyHtmlCaption":"Google’s spam cop, Matt Cutts, was not pleased about a campaign to make JCPenney.com seem more popular.","crops({\"renditionNames\":[\"articleLarge\",\"jumbo\",\"superJumbo\",\"articleInline\",\"popup\",\"mobileMasterAt3x\"]})":[{"type":"id","generated":true,"id":"Image:SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvN2NmODY3OGUtNTA1YS01NTVjLTg5MjAtODhiOTAwZGExYmZk.crops({\"renditionNames\":[\"articleLarge\",\"jumbo\",\"superJumbo\",\"articleInline\",\"popup\",\"mobileMasterAt3x\"]}).0","typename":"ImageCrop"},{"type":"id","generated":true,"id":"Image:SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvN2NmODY3OGUtNTA1YS01NTVjLTg5MjAtODhiOTAwZGExYmZk.crops({\"renditionNames\":[\"articleLarge\",\"jumbo\",\"superJumbo\",\"articleInline\",\"popup\",\"mobileMasterAt3x\"]}).1","typename":"ImageCrop"}],"caption":{"type":"id","generated":true,"id":"$Image:SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvN2NmODY3OGUtNTA1YS01NTVjLTg5MjAtODhiOTAwZGExYmZk.caption","typename":"TextOnlyDocumentBlock"},"__typename":"Image"},"ImageRendition:images20110213businessJP-SEARCH-2JP-SEARCH-2-jumbo.jpg":{"url":"http:\u002F\u002Fweb.archive.org\u002Fweb\u002F20210503143815\u002Fhttps:\u002F\u002Fstatic01.nyt.com\u002Fimages\u002F2011\u002F02\u002F13\u002Fbusiness\u002FJP-SEARCH-2\u002FJP-SEARCH-2-jumbo.jpg","name":"jumbo","width":1024,"height":673,"__typename":"ImageRendition"},"ImageRendition:images20110213businessJP-SEARCH-2JP-SEARCH-2-popup.jpg":{"url":"http:\u002F\u002Fweb.archive.org\u002Fweb\u002F20210503143815\u002Fhttps:\u002F\u002Fstatic01.nyt.com\u002Fimages\u002F2011\u002F02\u002F13\u002Fbusiness\u002FJP-SEARCH-2\u002FJP-SEARCH-2-popup.jpg","name":"popup","width":650,"height":427,"__typename":"ImageRendition"},"Image:SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvN2NmODY3OGUtNTA1YS01NTVjLTg5MjAtODhiOTAwZGExYmZk.crops({\"renditionNames\":[\"articleLarge\",\"jumbo\",\"superJumbo\",\"articleInline\",\"popup\",\"mobileMasterAt3x\"]}).0":{"renditions":[{"type":"id","generated":false,"id":"ImageRendition:images20110213businessJP-SEARCH-2JP-SEARCH-2-jumbo.jpg","typename":"ImageRendition"},{"type":"id","generated":false,"id":"ImageRendition:images20110213businessJP-SEARCH-2JP-SEARCH-2-popup.jpg","typename":"ImageRendition"}],"__typename":"ImageCrop"},"ImageRendition:images20110213businessJP-SEARCH-2JP-SEARCH-2-articleInline.jpg":{"url":"http:\u002F\u002Fweb.archive.org\u002Fweb\u002F20210503143815\u002Fhttps:\u002F\u002Fstatic01.nyt.com\u002Fimages\u002F2011\u002F02\u002F13\u002Fbusiness\u002FJP-SEARCH-2\u002FJP-SEARCH-2-articleInline.jpg","name":"articleInline","width":190,"height":252,"__typename":"ImageRendition"},"Image:SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvN2NmODY3OGUtNTA1YS01NTVjLTg5MjAtODhiOTAwZGExYmZk.crops({\"renditionNames\":[\"articleLarge\",\"jumbo\",\"superJumbo\",\"articleInline\",\"popup\",\"mobileMasterAt3x\"]}).1":{"renditions":[{"type":"id","generated":false,"id":"ImageRendition:images20110213businessJP-SEARCH-2JP-SEARCH-2-articleInline.jpg","typename":"ImageRendition"}],"__typename":"ImageCrop"},"$Image:SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvN2NmODY3OGUtNTA1YS01NTVjLTg5MjAtODhiOTAwZGExYmZk.caption":{"text":"Google’s spam cop, Matt Cutts, was not pleased about a campaign to make JCPenney.com seem more popular.","__typename":"TextOnlyDocumentBlock"},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.111.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"“You do pretty well,” he wrote, referring to income from his links trading. “The thing is, the more you invest (time and money) the better results you get. Right now I get enough to buy myself new test devices for my Android apps (like $150\u002Fmonth) with zero effort. I have to do nothing. Ads just sit there and if people click, I make money.”","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.113.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Efforts to reach TNX itself last week via e-mail were not successful.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.114.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Interviewing a purveyor of black-hat services face-to-face was a considerable undertaking. They are a low-profile bunch. But a link-selling specialist named Mark Stevens — who says he had nothing to do with the Penney link effort — agreed to chat. He did so on the condition that his company not be named, a precaution he justified by recounting what happened when the company apparently angered Google a few months ago.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.116.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"“It was my fault,” Mr. Stevens said. “I posted a job opening on a Stanford Engineering alumni mailing list, and mentioned the name of our company and a brief description of what we do. I think some Google employees saw it.”","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.118.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"In a matter of days, the company could not be found in a Google search.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.119.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"“Literally, you typed the name of the company into the search box and we did not turn up. Anywhere. You’d find us if you knew our Web address. But in terms of search, we just disappeared.”","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.121.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"The company now operates under a new name and with a profile that is low even in the building where it claims to have an office. The landlord at the building, a gleaming, glassy midrise next to Route 101 in Redwood City, Calif., said she had never heard of the company.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.123.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Mr. Stevens agreed to meet in mid-January for a dinner paid for by The Times. Asked to pick a “fine restaurant” in his neighborhood, he rather cheekily selected a modern French bistro in Palo Alto offering an eight-course prix fixe meal for $118. Liquid nitrogen and “fairy tale pumpkin” were two of the featured ingredients.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.125.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Mr. Stevens turned out to be a boyish-looking 31-year-old native of Singapore. (Stevens is the name he uses for work; he says he has a Chinese last name, which he did not share.) He speaks with a slight accent and in an animated hush, like a man worried about eavesdroppers. He describes his works with the delighted, mischievous grin of a sophomore who just hid a stink bomb.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.127.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"“The key is to roll the campaign out slowly,” he said as he nibbled at seared duck foie gras. “A lot of companies are in a rush. They want as many links as we can get them as fast as possible. But Google will spot that. It will flag a Web site that goes from zero links to a few hundred in a week.”","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.129.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"The hardest part about the link-selling business, he explained, is signing up deep-pocketed mainstream clients. Lots of them, it seems, are afraid they’ll get caught. Another difficulty is finding quality sites to post links. Whoever set up the JCPenney.com campaign, he said, relied on some really low-rent, spammy sites — the kind with low PageRanks, as Google calls its patented measure of a site’s quality. The higher the PageRank, the more “Google juice” a site offers others to which it is linked.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.131.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"“The sites that TNX uses mostly have low PageRanks,” Mr. Stevens said.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.132.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Mr. Stevens said that Web site owners, or publishers, as he calls them, get a small fee for each link, and the transaction is handled entirely over the Web.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.134.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Publishers can reject certain keywords and links — Mr. Stevens said some balked at a lingerie link — but for the most part the system is on a kind of autopilot. A client pays Mr. Stevens and his colleagues for links, which are then farmed out to Web sites. Payment to publishers is handled via PayPal.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.136.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"You might expect Mr. Stevens to have a certain amount of contempt for Google, given that he spends his professional life finding ways to subvert it. But through the evening he mentioned a few times that he’s in awe of the company, and the quality of its search engine.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.138.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"So how does he justify all his efforts to undermine that engine?","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.139.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"“I think we need to make a distinction between two different kinds of searches — informational and commercial,” he said. “If you search ‘cancer,’ that’s an informational search and on those, Google is amazing. But in commercial searches, Google’s results are really polluted. My own personal experience says that the guy with the biggest S.E.O. budget always ranks the highest.”","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.141.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"To Mr. Stevens, S.E.O. is a game, and if you’re not paying black hats, you are losing to rivals with fewer compunctions.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.143.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"WHY did Google fail to catch a campaign that had been under way for months? One, no less, that benefited a company that Google had already taken action against three times? And one that relied on a collection of Web sites that were not exactly hiding their spamminess?","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.145.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Mr. Cutts emphasized that there are 200 million domain names and a mere 24,000 employees at Google.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.146.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"“Spammers never stop,” he said. Battling those spammers is a never-ending job, and one that he believes Google keeps getting better and better at.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.148.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Here’s another hypothesis, this one for the conspiracy-minded. Last year, Advertising Age obtained a Google document that listed some of its largest advertisers, including AT&T, eBay and yes, J. C. Penney. The company, this document said, spent $2.46 million a month on paid Google search ads — the kind you see next to organic results.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.150.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Is it possible that Google was willing to countenance an extensive black-hat campaign because it helped one of its larger advertisers? It’s the sort of question that European Union officials are now studying in an investigation of possible antitrust abuses by Google.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.152.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Investigators have been asking advertisers in Europe questions like this: “Please explain whether and, if yes, to what extent your advertising spending with Google has ever had an influence on your ranking in Google’s natural search.” And: “Has Google ever mentioned to you that increasing your advertising spending could improve your ranking in Google’s natural search?”","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.154.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Asked if Penney received any breaks because of the money it has spent on ads, Mr. Cutts said, “I’ll give a categorical denial.” He then made an impassioned case for Google’s commitment to separating the money side of the business from the search side. The former has zero influence on the latter, he said.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.156.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"“If you asked me for the names of five people in advertising engineering, I don’t think I could give you the names,” he said. “There is a very long history at Google of saying ‘We are not going to worry about short-term revenue.’ ” He added: “We rely on the trust of our users. We realize the responsibility that we have to our users.”","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.158.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"He noted, too, that before The Times presented evidence of the paid links to JCPenney.com, Google had just begun to roll out an algorithm change that had a negative effect on Penney’s search results. (The tweak affected “how we trust links,” Mr. Cutts said, declining to elaborate.)","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.160.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"True, JCPenney.com’s showing in Google searches had declined slightly by Feb. 8, as the algorithm change began to take effect. In “comforter sets,” Penney went from No. 1 to No. 7. In “sweater dresses,” from No. 1 to No. 10.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.162.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"But the real damage to Penney’s results began when Google started that “manual action.” The decline can be charted: On Feb. 1, the average Penney position for 59 search terms was 1.3.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.164.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"On Feb. 8, when the algorithm was changing, it was 4.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.165.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"By Feb. 10, it was 52.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.166.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"MR. CUTTS said he did not plan to write about Penney’s situation, as ","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.166.content.1":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"he did","formats":[{"type":"id","generated":true,"id":"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.166.content.1.formats.0","typename":"LinkFormat"}]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.166.content.1.formats.0":{"__typename":"LinkFormat","url":"http:\u002F\u002Fweb.archive.org\u002Fweb\u002F20210503143815\u002Fhttp:\u002F\u002Fwww.mattcutts.com\u002Fblog\u002Framping-up-on-international-webspam\u002F","title":"The 2006 post."},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.166.content.2":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":" with BMW in 2006. Rarely, he explained, does he single out a company publicly, because Google’s goal is to preserve the integrity of results, not to embarrass people.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.168.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"“But just because we don’t talk about it,” he said, “doesn’t mean we won’t take strong action.”","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzE2OGJlZjA5LTg3ZGItNTlhNS04OTkxLTlmNDk5MGNmMTY0Mw==.storylines.0.storyline":{"uri":"nyt:\u002F\u002Fstoryline\u002F88ef95a0-10f4-4ab7-80ce-6eabc324fea3","displayName":"Today
